statement.p: In a race, a car drove through the starting line at the speed of 10 meters per second. It was accelerating at 1 meters per second squared. Its distance from the starting position can be modeled by the function d(t)={0.5t^{2}+10t}\text{.} Find the answer using technology.
After seconds, the car was {94.5} meters away from the starting position.

solution.p: Use technology to graph the functions d(t)={0.5t^{2}+10t} and y={94.5}\text{,} and find the point of intersection where x is positive, (7,{94.5})\text{.}
According to the point of intersection, the car was {94.5} meters away from the starting position after 7 seconds.
